summary: PR #813 introduced an API v1 distributed relay bridge that routed plaintext OpenAI-style messages via relay-owned state, violating relay-blind E2EE until Prompt 1 containment, Prompt 2 E2EE restoration, and Prompt 3 safeguards landed.
impact: High-severity code-level plaintext exposure risk existed in distributed API v1 relay handling; no confirmed real-world exploitation is documented in repository evidence.
fix: Prompt 1 (PR #834) fail-closed unsafe distributed API v1 relay dispatch, Prompt 2 (PR #836) restored distributed relay via E2EE envelopes, and Prompt 3 (PR #842) added static/runtime/network/log/contract/docs safeguards.
lessons: Protocol bridge refactors must preserve relay-blind E2EE invariants end-to-end, and security-critical invariants need layered static and runtime guardrails before rollout.
